surface.name: OpenAI Responses API, JavaScript developer quickstart
surface.selection_basis: OpenAI's official Developer quickstart explicitly says to create an API key and run a first API call. Its first language tab is JavaScript and the documented terminal signal is printed model output. This establishes the boundary without selecting it independently.

alternatives_considered.name: Python quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The same official page offers an equivalent Python tab; JavaScript is the first displayed route, so only local package and run commands differ.

alternatives_considered.name: Agents SDK quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is a later, more specialized agent path; the general Developer quickstart first establishes a basic Responses API call.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create credentials and receive output from a first OpenAI API request.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has arrived at OpenAI's official Developer quickstart.
The developer has no configured API key, SDK package, or example file yet.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A JavaScript program sends the documented Responses API request and prints the model-generated text.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Running `node example.mjs` prints the output text; the page then says `Congrats on running a free test API request!`.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The quickstart's heading is `Install the OpenAI SDK and Run an API Call`, followed by a printed response and a congratulations message for the free test request.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: It is the first complete authenticated API interaction explicitly established by the general official quickstart, before billing credits and more specialized capabilities.

prerequisites.requirement: Access to the OpenAI Platform dashboard is required to create an API key.
A server-side JavaScript runtime such as Node.js, Deno, or Bun and an npm-compatible package manager are needed for the selected tab.

primary_path.action: Create an API key in the OpenAI dashboard and store it securely.
details: The quickstart warns to keep the key in a safe location.
primary_path.success_signal: The key is generated and copied to secure local storage.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The page does not document a manual approval wait.

primary_path.action: Export the key as `OPENAI_API_KEY` in the terminal.
details: The macOS and Linux example uses `export`; PowerShell uses `setx`.
OpenAI SDKs read this environment variable automatically.
primary_path.output: API key available to the SDK process.
primary_path.success_signal: The environment variable is set for the runtime.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Shell persistence and process refresh behavior depend on the operating system.

primary_path.action: Install the official JavaScript SDK with `npm install openai`.
details: The selected tab supports server-side Node.js, Deno, or Bun environments.
primary_path.success_signal: The `openai` package is available to import.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Package installation requires network and filesystem access.

primary_path.action: Create `example.mjs` with the documented Responses API example.
details: Import OpenAI and instantiate the client.
Call `client.responses.create` with the documented model and one-sentence bedtime-story input, then log `response.output_text`.
primary_path.success_signal: The file contains the client, request, and print statements.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Incorrect environment loading or a missing key prevents authentication.

details: The SDK authenticates from the environment and calls the Responses API.
primary_path.input: Runnable example and configured API key.
primary_path.output: Model response object and printed output text.
primary_path.success_signal: A generated one-sentence story appears in the terminal and the quickstart's congratulations state is reached.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The docs say output should appear in a few moments; API errors are not detailed in this quickstart section.

branches.path: Use `setx OPENAI_API_KEY "your_api_key_here"`, then continue with the selected SDK runtime.

branches.condition: The developer selects Python, .NET, Java, Go, or Ruby.
branches.path: Use the corresponding official SDK install, example code, and runtime command in the parallel language tab.
branches.effect_on_first_success: All routes end at a first Responses API output, but local tooling differs.

friction_gates.description: The developer needs dashboard access to create the API key.
A secret API key must be created, copied, stored safely, and exposed to the SDK without embedding it in code.
The official SDK must be installed for the selected language.

sources.title: Developer quickstart
sections_used: Create and export an API key
Install the OpenAI SDK and Run an API Call
evidence_supported: JavaScript install, file, request, run, and printed-output signals
Free-test congratulations and post-success billing step

uncertainties.question: What account states receive the free test API request referenced by the quickstart?
uncertainties.impact: Some developers may encounter a billing or credit gate before the same terminal response.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The quickstart congratulates the reader for a free test and places Add credits afterward, but does not define eligibility conditions on this page.

excluded_after_success: Production key management, retries, monitoring, and cost controls
